Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental. A `surprise` marks where your world model is wrong -- re-probe there instead of pressing on.

Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key findings:
1. Orange block is 2x5 (rows [40,41] cols [34,38]), blue block is 3x5 (rows [42,44] cols [34,38]). Together they form a 5x5 block.
2. RIGHT from [34,38] was blocked (stayed at [34,38]).
3. UP from [34,38] worked to [35,36].
4. RIGHT from [35,36] was blocked (stayed at [35,36]).
5. UP from [35,36] worked to [30,31].
6. RIGHT from [30,31] worked to [39,43].
So from [30,31] cols [34,38], RIGHT works to [39,43]. But from [35,36] cols [34,38], RIGHT was blocked.
Current position: rows [30,31] cols [39,43]
From earlier exploration:
- From [39,43], DOWN leads to [40,41] cols [29,33] (center)
- From [39,43], RIGHT was blocked
- From [39,43], UP was not tried
Let me try UP from [39,43] to see if there's a path upward on the right side.
Also, the white cross is at rows [46,47] cols [51,52]. Looking at the gray structure boundary:
- [39, 54], [39, 58], [54, 58], [54, 39], [50, 39], [50, 43], [49, 44], [40, 44], [40, 48], [39, 49]
This suggests there's a room on the right side around rows [39,54] cols [44,58]. The white cross at [46,51] is inside this room.
The boundary shows:
- [39, 54] to [39, 58]: top edge of right room
- [39, 58] to [54, 58]: right edge
- [54, 58] to [54, 39]: bottom edge
- [54, 39] to [50, 39]: bottom-left edge
- [50, 39] to [50, 43]: left edge going up
- [50, 43] to [49, 44]: diagonal
- [49, 44] to [40, 44]: left edge going up
- [40, 44] to [40, 48]: top edge going right
- [40, 48] to [39, 49]: diagonal
So the right room has an opening around rows [40,49] cols [44,48]. The blocks need to enter this room to reach the white cross.
From [39,43], the blocks are at cols [39,43]. The room opening is at cols [44,48]. So the blocks need to move RIGHT to enter the room. But earlier, RIGHT from [39,43] was blocked.
Wait, let me reconsider. The blocks are 5x5, so they need a 5x5 opening. The room opening might be too narrow.
Actually, looking at the boundary more carefully:
- [40, 44] to [40, 48]: this is a horizontal segment at row 40, cols 44-48
- [39, 49] connects to this
The opening seems to be around rows [39,40] cols [44,49]. The blocks at rows [30,31] cols [39,43] are above this opening. To enter the room, the blocks need to be at rows [40,44] (or similar) and cols [44,48].
Let me try a different approach: from [39,43], go DOWN to [40,41] cols [29,33], then find a path to the right room.
Wait, but DOWN from [39,43] led to [40,41] cols [29,33], which is the center. From there, I can go RIGHT to [34,38], then UP to [30,31], then RIGHT to [39,43]. This is the loop I've been exploring.
Let me try UP from [39,43] to see if there's a path upward on the right side that leads to the white cross room.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: rows [30,31] cols [39,43]
# White cross at rows [46,47] cols [51,52]
# Gray structure boundary shows right room around rows [39,54] cols [44,58]
# Opening at rows [39,40] cols [44,49]
#
# Try UP from [39,43] to explore upper-right path

predict(expect_change=True, note='UP from [39,43]')
action(['UP'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try RIGHT
predict(expect_change=True, note='RIGHT')
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try DOWN
predict(expect_change=True, note='DOWN')
action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Try DOWN again
predict(expect_change=True, note='DOWN again')
action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Orange: rows [{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Check white cross proximity
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
orange_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'O']
if white_nodes and orange_nodes:
    w = white_nodes[0]
    o = orange_nodes[0]
    w_rows = [p[0] for p in w['boundary']]
    w_cols = [p[1] for p in w['boundary']]
    o_rows = [p[0] for p in o['boundary']]
    o_cols = [p[1] for p in o['boundary']]
    print(f"White: rows [{min(w_rows)},{max(w_rows)}] cols [{min(w_cols)},{max(w_cols)}]")
    print(f"Orange: rows [{min(o_rows)},{max(o_rows)}] cols [{min(o_cols)},{max(o_cols)}]")
    print(f"Row diff: {min(w_rows) - max(o_rows)}, Col diff: {min(w_cols) - max(o_cols)}")

print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
